core_behavior: Monitor database performance metrics, such as query execution times, disk usage, memory usage, and CPU utilization.
Analyze database schema, indexing, and query patterns to identify potential performance issues.

Apply optimization techniques: indexing and re-indexing tables, reorganizing and re-partitioning data, optimizing query execution plans, caching frequently accessed data, implementing connection pooling and load balancing.
Provide recommendations for database configuration changes, such as adjusting buffer pool sizes, modifying query timeout settings, and optimizing database parameter settings.
inputs: Database connection parameters (e.g., database name, username, password, host, port)
Database schema information (e.g., table structures, relationships, indexing)
Performance metrics data (e.g., query execution times, disk usage, memory usage, CPU utilization)
User-defined optimization goals and constraints (e.g., prioritize query performance, minimize storage usage)
outputs: Performance optimization recommendations, including indexing and re-indexing suggestions, query optimization plans, database configuration changes, and caching and connection pooling recommendations.
Performance metrics and analysis reports, including query execution time distributions, disk usage and memory usage trends, and CPU utilization patterns.
Database schema and configuration changes, applied automatically or manually based on user preferences.
assumptions: The system will support relational databases (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle) and NoSQL databases (e.g., MongoDB, Cassandra).
The system will use a combination of static analysis and dynamic monitoring to identify performance issues.
The system will provide a web-based interface for users to input optimization goals and constraints, view performance metrics and analysis reports, and apply optimization recommendations.
The system will use machine learning algorithms to predict performance bottlenecks and recommend optimization strategies.
failure_modes: Database connection failures: The system will retry database connections and provide error messages to the user.
Insufficient permissions: The system will provide error messages to the user and request elevated permissions to perform optimization tasks.
Inconsistent or incomplete database schema information: The system will use heuristics to infer missing information or request user input to resolve inconsistencies.
Optimization recommendation failures: The system will provide error messages to the user and offer alternative optimization strategies.
edge_cases: Very large databases: The system will use sampling techniques to analyze performance metrics and provide optimization recommendations.
Highly concurrent databases: The system will use locking mechanisms to ensure consistent analysis and optimization results.
Databases with complex schema: The system will use graph-based analysis techniques to identify performance issues and recommend optimization strategies.
Databases with limited resources: The system will provide optimization recommendations that balance performance and resource utilization.
security_constraints: The system will use secure authentication and authorization mechanisms to ensure only authorized users can access and modify database performance optimization settings.
The system will encrypt sensitive database connection parameters and performance metrics data.
The system will implement role-based access control to restrict user access to optimization features and performance metrics data.
performance_constraints: The system will provide optimization recommendations and performance metrics analysis within 1 minute of user input.
The system will handle up to 100 concurrent database connections and provide optimization recommendations for each connection.
The system will use no more than 10% of available CPU and memory resources to perform optimization tasks.
The system will support optimization of databases with up to 100 GB of storage and 100,000 concurrent users.
